title: monardella odoratissima subsp. glauca
intro: Monardella odoratissima subsp. glauca, also known as Purple Monardella or Purple Coyote Mint, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Monardella odoratissima subsp. glauca include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and supporting immune function, while also exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. This plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
